title: acinos thymoides var. villosus
intro: Acinos thymoides var. villosus, also known as hairy acinos, is a variant of the Acinos thymoides plant, a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of hairy acinos have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, treating digestive issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Hairy acinos can be prepared as an herbal infusion or decoction, and its essential oil can be extracted through steam distillation for use in aromatherapy and other applications.
